principle.name: Follow real repository conventions
principle.description: Base new widget work on the conventions already present in the widgets directory instead of inventing a new structure for every request.
principle.rationale: Existing widgets provide the most reliable reference for naming, file layout, and implementation style in this repository.

principle.name: Keep widget responsibilities focused
principle.description: Build the widget, its metadata, preview files, and widget-local documentation, but do not drift into TWX packaging or deployment workflows.
principle.rationale: Clear boundaries reduce accidental scope expansion and make handoff to the packaging mode explicit.

principle.name: Keep implementation and documentation synchronized
principle.description: Whenever data bindings, config options, events, or preview behavior change, update the corresponding documentation and metadata files in the same task.
principle.rationale: Widget projects become difficult to maintain when README, datamodel, event notes, and implementation files diverge.

convention.rule: Use PascalCase for widget names and keep file naming consistent with surrounding widgets
Use semantic HTML and accessibility attributes in Layout.html

examples.good: Use meaningful landmarks, labels, and ARIA attributes where needed
examples.bad: Use generic nested div elements without accessible structure

convention.rule: Scope styles to the widget and avoid collisions with other coach views

examples.good: Use widget-specific class names and keep styles local to the widget
examples.bad: Use broad selectors that can affect unrelated widgets

convention.rule: Use Carbon Design System values with inline documentation comments

critical_note.title: Carbon Theme Integration
critical_note.description: BAW widget InlineCSS.css files are pure CSS, NOT LESS. They do not support LESS preprocessing or variable substitution. Therefore, you MUST use hardcoded values with inline comments that document which Carbon theme variables they correspond to.
critical_note.rationale: This approach provides documentation while maintaining compatibility with BAW's CSS compilation process. The comments make it easy to update values when the theme changes and ensure consistency across all widgets.

font_family.value: 'IBM Plex Sans', 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

reference_documentation.doc: docs/CARBON_THEME_INTEGRATION.md - Integration guide with best practices

examples.good: Always include font-family with Carbon theme comment Use exact Carbon color values with variable name in comment Document font sizes with their theme variable names Reference Gray scale colors with their number (Gray 20, Gray 70, etc.)
examples.bad: Use LESS variables (@bpm-color-primary) directly in CSS Use arbitrary colors not from Carbon palette Omit theme reference comments Use generic font families without IBM Plex Sans

convention.rule: Use BAW widget data and option access patterns consistently

critical_note.title: List Binding Data Access
critical_note.description: When the widget's bindingType has isList:true in config.json, BAW automatically wraps the array data in an object with an 'items' property. You MUST access the data using this.getData().items, NOT this.getData() directly or any custom property name.
critical_note.rationale: BAW's data binding system uses a standardized wrapper for list data. The .items property is the only way to access the actual array when isList:true is set. This is a BAW framework convention, not configurable.

examples.good: Read values through the established widget APIs and keep event execution explicit
examples.bad: Mix unrelated runtime assumptions into inline widget logic

convention.rule: Keep config and business object definitions aligned with the actual widget behavior

examples.good: Document real options, real events, and real business object references
examples.bad: Leave stale metadata after changing widget behavior

convention.rule: ALWAYS integrate advance preview files with BAW's preview system
convention.description: Preview files must follow the BAW preview pattern using the mixObject structure and integrate with BPMExt-Controls.preview.js layer. Never create standalone preview implementations.

required_structure.file: Contains styles and minimal container structure. Preview content is generated by JavaScript, not hardcoded in HTML.

Implements mixObject with: - createPreview(containingDiv, labelText, callback) - getLabelDomElement() - generateSampleData(domConstruct, domAttr, domClass) - propertyChanged(propertyName, propertyValue) - modelChanged(propertyName, propertyValue)

examples.good: Use Dojo modules (dojo/dom-construct, dojo/dom-class, dojo/dom-attr) Integrate with bpmui/preview/BPMExt-Core-Designer Generate preview content dynamically in generateSampleData() Handle config option changes in propertyChanged()
examples.bad: Create standalone JavaScript functions without BAW integration Hardcode preview content directly in HTML Use vanilla JavaScript without Dojo modules Skip propertyChanged() implementation

reference_widgets.widget: DateOutput - Simple preview with formatted date display
ProgressBar - Preview with dynamic progress visualization
Stepper - Complex preview with multiple interactive elements

pitfall.description: Encoding packaging logic inside the widget mode
pitfall.why_problematic: Packaging rules belong to the package manager mode, not the widget authoring mode.
pitfall.correct_approach: Stop at widget implementation and explicitly hand off when packaging or deployment is requested.

pitfall.description: Documenting an idealized structure that does not match the repository
pitfall.why_problematic: If the rules differ from the real project, the mode will generate misleading guidance.
pitfall.correct_approach: Use the actual widgets in the repository as the source of truth and note inconsistencies when needed.

pitfall.why_problematic: Partial widget structures make later validation and packaging harder.
pitfall.correct_approach: Ensure the requested widget includes the files needed for its intended runtime behavior and documentation.

category.name: before_starting
category.item: Check similar widgets for patterns before creating new files
Confirm whether the task is widget authoring rather than packaging

category.name: during_implementation
category.item: Keep naming consistent across files and metadata
Align implementation, preview files, and documentation

category.name: before_completion
category.item: Verify that the widget scope has not expanded into packaging
Confirm any new metadata or documentation reflects actual behavior
